text:
<think>I should start by clarifying how long it takes John to solve an extreme sudoku puzzle, since it is 4 times the time of a normal sudoku.</think>
A normal sudoku puzzle takes John 45 minutes, so an extreme sudoku takes 4 × 45 = 180 minutes.
<think>Now, I need to determine how many half-hour intervals are in 180 minutes, since he drinks a bottle every half hour.</think>
There are 60 minutes in an hour, so 180 minutes is 3 hours.
<think>I should now calculate how many half-hour (30 minute) intervals are in 3 hours.</think>
There are 3 hours × 2 = 6 half-hour intervals in 3 hours.
<think>Since John drinks a bottle of water every half hour, he will drink one bottle per interval.</think>
Therefore, John drinks 6 bottles of water while solving an extreme sudoku puzzle.
<think>I should now present the final answer after the required #### marker.</think>
#### 6
